title: Getting Started with phscs
text: Install CRAN: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("phscs") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("yng-me/phscs")

title: Getting Started with phscs
text: classification functions share parameters: Every classification description column available via cols = "description":
code: get_psic(level = "sections", cols = "description") |>   head(5) |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left")

title: Getting Started with phscs
text: get_psgc() re-exported psgc package. full walkthrough — releases, crosswalks, population data — see vignette("psgc", package = "psgc"). Use geographic_level filter specific tier: Use shorten_region_name() compact long region labels plots tables: filter rows code, use standard R subsetting call:
code: psgc_regions <- get_psgc(geographic_level = "region")  psgc_regions |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left") psgc_regions |>   shorten_region_name(which = "label") |>   head(5) |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left") # All areas whose PSGC code starts with "10" (Region X) psgc_all <- get_psgc() psgc_region_10 <- psgc_all[grepl("^10", psgc_all$psgc_code), ]

title: Getting Started with phscs
text: PSIC organises economic activities sections, divisions, groups, classes, sub-classes. default returns detailed level.
code: get_psic() |>   head(10) |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left") get_psic(level = "sections") |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left")

title: Getting Started with phscs
text: PSOC classifies occupations major, sub-major, minor, unit groups. default returns unit groups.
code: get_psoc() |>   head(10) |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left") get_psoc(level = "major") |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left")

title: Getting Started with phscs
text: PSCED classifies fields study education levels, broad fields, narrow fields, detailed fields. default returns detailed fields.
code: get_psced() |>   head(10) |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left") get_psced(level = "levels") |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left")

what: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP)
title: Getting Started with phscs
text: PCOICOP classifies household expenditures. Two editions available: "2020" (default, 3,472 items) "2009" (1,722 items). Switch 2009 edition version = "2009":
code: get_pcoicop() |>   head(10) |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left") get_pcoicop(level = "divisions") |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left") get_pcoicop(version = "2009", level = "divisions") |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left")

title: Getting Started with phscs
text: PCPC classifies goods services. default returns 8,734 items across six levels.
code: get_pcpc() |>   head(10) |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left") get_pcpc(level = "sections") |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left")

title: Getting Started with phscs
text: PSCCS classifies commodities sections, divisions, groups, classes, sub-classes. default returns 1,870 items.
code: get_psccs() |>   head(10) |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left") get_psccs(level = "section") |>   gt::gt() |>   gt::tab_options(table.align = "left")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Bhas Abdulsamad. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Abdulsamad B (2026). phscs: Philippine Statistical Classification Systems. R package version 0.2.0, https://yng-.github.io/phscs/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {phscs: Philippine Statistical Classification Systems},   author = {Bhas Abdulsamad},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://yng-me.github.io/phscs/}, }

title: Philippine Statistical Classification Systems
text: phscs provides single, consistent interface seven Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) classification systems. data bundled package — internet connection API token required.

title: Philippine Statistical Classification Systems
text: Install CRAN: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("phscs") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("yng-me/phscs")

title: Philippine Statistical Classification Systems
text: classification functions share interface: full walkthrough every classification examples, see:
code: library(phscs)  # Geographic data — re-exported from the psgc package get_psgc(geographic_level = "region") |> head(3) #>       psgc_code                  area_name correspondence_code geographic_level #> 1    0100000000   Region I (Ilocos Region)           010000000              Reg #> 3398 0200000000 Region II (Cagayan Valley)           020000000              Reg #> 5808 0300000000 Region III (Central Luzon)           030000000              Reg #>      old_name city_class income_classification urban_rural island_region #> 1        <NA>       <NA>                  <NA>        <NA>             L #> 3398     <NA>       <NA>                  <NA>        <NA>             L #> 5808     <NA>       <NA>                  <NA>        <NA>             L  # Industrial classification (default: sub-classes) get_psic() |> head(3) #>   value #> 1 01111 #> 2 01112 #> 3 01113 #>                                                                                                                                               label #> 1 Growing of leguminous crops such as: mongo, string beans (sitao), pigeon peas, gisantes, garbanzos, bountiful beans (habichuelas), peas (sitsaro) #> 2                                                                                                                             Growing of groundnuts #> 3                                     Growing of oil seeds (except groundnuts) such as soya beans, sunflower and growing of other oil seeds, n.e.c.  # Occupational classification (default: unit groups) get_psoc() |> head(3) #>   value                                    label #> 1  1111                              Legislators #> 2  1112              Senior government officials #> 3  1113 Traditional chiefs and heads of villages # Choose a level of detail get_psic(level = "sections") get_psoc(level = "major") get_pcoicop(level = "divisions")  # Include the full text description get_psic(level = "sections", cols = "description")  # Switch to an older edition (where available) get_pcoicop(version = "2009") vignette("phscs")

what: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP) — get_pcoicop
title: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP) — get_pcoicop
text: Philippine Classification Individual Consumption According Purpose (PCOICOP)

title: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP) — get_pcoicop
code: get_pcoicop(version = NULL, level = NULL, minimal = TRUE, cols = NULL)

title: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP) — get_pcoicop
text: version Character. Version PCOICOP dataset. Default latest available ("2020"). Use "2009" 2009 edition. level Character. Classification level: "", "divisions", "groups", "class", "sub-class", "item", "subitem" (default). minimal Logical. TRUE (default), returns value label columns. cols Optional character vector additional columns include ("description" extra column available).

title: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP) — get_pcoicop
text: data frame PCOICOP classifications.

title: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP) — get_pcoicop
text: https://psa.gov.ph/classification/pcoicop

title: Philippine Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (PCOICOP) — get_pcoicop
code: pcoicop <- get_pcoicop() pcoicop_divisions <- get_pcoicop(level = "divisions")

title: Philippine Standard Geographic Code (PSGC) — get_psgc
text: Re-exported psgc package. See   get_psgc full documentation.

title: Philippine Standard Geographic Code (PSGC) — get_psgc
code: get_psgc(   release = latest_release(),   geographic_level = NULL,   include_population_data = FALSE )

title: Philippine Standard Geographic Code (PSGC) — get_psgc
text: release release name [list_releases()]. Defaults [latest_release()]. geographic_level character vector geographic levels filter . Accepts canonical codes (`"Reg"`, `"Prov"`, `"City"`, `"Mun"`, `"SubMun"`, `"Bgy"`) well common aliases `"Region"`, `"Province"`, `"Municipality"`, `"Barangay"`, `"Sub-Municipality"`, etc. Use `"city_mun"` (aliases like `"City-Municipality"`) include cities municipalities. `NULL` (default) returns levels. include_population_data Logical. `TRUE`, census population figures joined onto result, adding `population` (integer) `year` columns. geographic unit produces one row per available census year. Defaults `FALSE`.

title: Philippine Standard Geographic Code (PSGC) — get_psgc
text: data frame PSGC geographic data.

title: Shorten region name — shorten_region_name
text: function shortens region names PSGC data frame.

title: Shorten region name — shorten_region_name
code: shorten_region_name(data, which = c("label", "number"), col = "area_name")

title: Shorten region name — shorten_region_name
text: data data frame containing PSGC data. Character. Specifies whether shorten region name label number. Options "label" "number". col Character. name column containing area names. Default "area_name".

title: Shorten region name — shorten_region_name
text: data frame region names shortened based specified   argument.

title: phscs 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-13

title: phscs 0.2.0
text: get_psgc() completely new interface. now re-exported directly psgc package. old parameters (token, version, level, harmonize, minimal, cols) gone. Use new psgc parameters instead: See vignette("psgc", package = "psgc") full documentation. ... (dplyr filter) removed classification functions. get_psic(), get_psoc(), get_pcpc(), get_psced(), get_pcoicop() longer accept unquoted filter expressions. Filter rows standard R subsetting call. token harmonize parameters removed classification functions. Data now always sourced bundled local dataset — API access internet connection needed.
code: # Before get_psgc(level = "regions") get_psgc(grepl("^10", area_code))   # dplyr filter  # After get_psgc(geographic_level = "region") psgc_all <- get_psgc() psgc_all[grepl("^10", psgc_all$psgc_code), ]   # base R subsetting

title: phscs 0.2.0
text: Added get_psccs() Philippine Standard Commodity Classification System (PSCCS 2018). classification functions gain cols = "description" option include full text description alongside value label. PCOICOP now ships 2020 (default) 2009 editions. Switch get_pcoicop(version = "2009").

title: phscs 0.2.0
text: Removed runtime dependencies dplyr, tidyr, purrr, stringr, jsonlite. Imports now cli psgc. Minimum R version lowered 4.1.0 3.5. Data pre-parsed build time compressed sysdata.rda, making functions substantially faster.

title: phscs 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-11-09 Initial CRAN submission.
